tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal expected movement over the next 16 months, with the median forecast at $77,975 (flat vs. spot). The interquartile range ($76,725–$79,015) suggests traders are pricing in roughly ±2.4% volatility, while the 10th–90th percentile range ($76,000–$79,663) indicates only ~2.4% downside and ~2.3% upside risk in the outer tails despite $3.3M in volume across 50 markets.

tenors.narrative: Prediction markets show minimal WTI price movement over the next 16 months, with a median of $96/bbl (just +0.4% from spot). The distribution is relatively tight around this level, with 50% probability between $85-$108/bbl, suggesting moderate consensus around current prices despite $4M in trading volume across 21 markets.

By mid-2026, prediction markets shift sharply bearish with a median of $77/bbl (-20.1% from spot), though this forecast carries significantly less conviction given only $410k volume across 11 markets. The wide interquartile range ($67-$134) and extreme 90th percentile of $166/bbl indicate substantial disagreement, with the market pricing in meaningful downside risk but not ruling out substantial upside scenarios.
